title: Computer Space: The First Arcade Game
story: Beep-boop! A new era of entertainment began in 1971. On October 15, the world's first arcade video game, Computer Space, made its debut at the MOA Show in the United States. Created by a company called Syzygy, this space-themed game paved the way for the colorful, noisy arcades we know today. Players could blast alien spaceships on a small screen, experiencing digital fun like never before!

title: Cassini's Epic Journey to Saturn Begins
story: Blast off! On October 15, 1997, the Cassini spacecraft roared into space, beginning its incredible voyage to Saturn. Cassini's mission would last nearly 20 years, revealing amazing secrets about Saturn, its moons, and its beautiful rings.

bonus_fact: Cassini discovered ocean worlds on Saturn's moons, potentially places where life could exist!

story: Zoom! NASA's Galileo spacecraft swooped incredibly close to Io, Jupiter's volcanic moon. On October 15, 2001, the robotic explorer passed within about 112 miles of Io's surface. title: The Day Pain Took a Nap
story: Imagine surgery without pain! Dr. William Thomas Green Morton changed medicine forever in 1846. title: Sprinting Queen Defends Her Crown
story: Wyomia Tyus raced into history on October 15, 1968. The American sprinter blazed across the finish line in Mexico City, clinching her second straight Olympic gold in the 100-meter dash. Tyus didn't just win—she shattered the world record with a lightning-fast time of 11.08 seconds. Her teammate Barbara Ferrell claimed silver, just a blink behind.

activity_choices: Her second straight Olympic gold in the 100-meter dash
A gold medal in long jump
activity_answer: Her second straight Olympic gold in the 100-meter dash
bonus_fact: Tyus was the first athlete, male or female, to win back-to-back Olympic golds in the 100-meter sprint.

title: The Great Calendar Leap
story: Imagine waking up and finding out you skipped 10 days! That's what happened in 1582 when the Gregorian calendar was introduced. Spain, Portugal, and the Papal States jumped from October 4 to October 15, fixing a problem with the old Julian calendar. This new system better matched the Earth's journey around the sun, keeping our dates in sync with the seasons.

title: New Zealand's Cricket Triumph in Nairobi
story: Cheers erupted at the Gymkhana Club in Nairobi as New Zealand clinched their first international cricket title. On October 15, 2000, the Kiwis soared to victory in the ICC Men's Cricket KnockOut Trophy, defeating India by 4 wickets. Chris Cairns emerged as the hero, scoring an unbeaten 102 runs off 113 balls. His stellar performance earned him the Man of the Match award and secured a historic win for New Zealand.
activity_question: Who was named Man of the Match in this cricket victory?
follow_up_question: How many runs did Chris Cairns score in the match?

title: New Teams Join the Big Leagues
story: Batter up! Baseball fans were buzzing with excitement in 1968. On October 15, two brand-new teams joined the American League. The Kansas City Royals and Seattle Pilots held a special draft to build their rosters. Each team picked 30 players from other clubs. The Royals chose pitcher Roger Nelson first, while the Pilots nabbed infielder Don Mincher. It was the start of a whole new ball game!
activity_question: Which two teams joined the American League in this story?
activity_choices: Kansas City Royals and Seattle Pilots
New York Yankees and Boston Red Sox
Chicago Cubs and St. Louis Cardinals
activity_answer: Kansas City Royals and Seattle Pilots
follow_up_question: How many players did each new team get to choose in the draft?

title: Hockey Hits the Small Screen
story: Pucks flew and fans cheered as the Madison Square Garden TV Network made its debut in 1969. On October 15, hockey lovers tuned in to watch the New York Rangers face off against the Minnesota North Stars. This special broadcast brought the excitement of live NHL action right into people's living rooms.
activity_question: Which two teams played in the debut broadcast of the Madison Square Garden TV Network?
activity_choices: New York Rangers and Minnesota North Stars
Detroit Red Wings and Toronto Maple Leafs
activity_answer: New York Rangers and Minnesota North Stars
bonus_fact: Madison Square Garden is a famous arena in New York City that hosts many sports and entertainment events.

title: Grand Ole Opry Hits the Small Screen
story: Yeehaw! Country music fans got a special treat in 1955. The famous Grand Ole Opry radio show made its television debut on October 15. ABC broadcast a live, hour-long show, bringing twangy tunes and cowboy boots right into living rooms across America. It was a big moment for country music, helping the genre reach even more listeners.
activity_question: What kind of music show made its television debut?
follow_up_question: Why do you think putting the Grand Ole Opry on TV was important for country music?
